title: DORA ICT Concentration ‚ÄĒ Regional Provider Outage Correlation Exposure Score

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.23' running on sourcetype provider:status and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚ÄĒ SME review required.

dataSources: `index=external` `sourcetype="provider:status"` (provider_id, region, impact_start, impact_end); `index=cloud` `sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset"` (provider_id, region, service_id, criticality)
app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), provider status RSS/JSON via HEC
spl:
index=external sourcetype="provider:status" impact_end="open" earliest=-2d
| join type=left provider_id, region [
    search index=cloud sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset" criticality="high"
    | stats dc(service_id) as exposed_services by provider_id, region
  ]
| where exposed_services>0
| table provider_id, region, impact_start, exposed_services

grandmaExplanation: When providers publish incident regions, correlating your asset footprint to those regions quantifies blast during external outages ‚ÄĒ evidence that geographic concentration is monitored, not assumed ‚ÄĒ so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), provider status RSS/JSON via HEC.
‚ÄĘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=external` `sourcetype="provider:status"` (provider_id, region, impact_start, impact_end); `index=cloud` `sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset"` (provider_id, region, service_id, criticality).
‚ÄĘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection
(1) Map status page regions to your tagging scheme; (2) tune for multi-region active-active; (3) integrate with major incident process; (4) store historical incidents for TLPT scenarios; (5) feed ICT concentration committee.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=external sourcetype="provider:status" impact_end="open" earliest=-2d
| join type=left provider_id, region [
    search index=cloud sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset" criticality="high"
    | stats dc(service_id) as exposed_services by provider_id, region
  ]
| where exposed_services>0
| table provider_id, region, impact_start, exposed_services
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA ICT Concentration ‚ÄĒ Regional Provider Outage Correlation Exposure Score** ‚ÄĒ When providers publish incident regions, correlating your asset footprint to those regions quantifies blast radius during external outages‚ÄĒevidence that geographic concentration is monitored, not assumed.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=external` `sourcetype="provider:status"` (provider_id, region, impact_start, impact_end); `index=cloud` `sourcetype="cmdb:cloud_asset"` (provider_id, region, service_id, criticality). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), provider status RSS/JSON via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚ÄĒrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: external; **sourcetype**: provider:status. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚ÄĘ Scopes the data: index=external, sourcetype="provider:status", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚ÄĘ Joins to a subsearch with `join` ‚ÄĒ set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
‚ÄĘ Filters the current rows with `where exposed_services>0` ‚ÄĒ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚ÄĘ Pipeline stage (see **DORA ICT Concentration ‚ÄĒ Regional Provider Outage Correlation Exposure Score**): table provider_id, region, impact_start, exposed_services


Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (live exposure), Map by region, Single value (exposed high services).
